terms.definition: An element of a vector space that can be added to other vectors and scaled by numbers while staying in the same space.
A set of vectors with addition and scalar multiplication operations that satisfy the standard vector space axioms.
A sum of the form alpha1v1 ... alphakvk where the alphas are scalars and the vs are vectors from the same vector space.

terms.term: Span of a set of vectors
terms.definition: The set of all linear combinations of a given collection of vectors the smallest subspace containing those vectors.
A subset of a vector space that itself forms a vector space under the same addition and scalar multiplication.
A linearly independent set of vectors that spans the entire vector space, giving unique coordinates for every vector.
The number of vectors in any basis of the space equivalently, the maximal number of linearly independent vectors in the space.
